title: malpighia undulata
intro: Malpighia undulata, also known as the Mammee or Wild Mango, is a tropical evergreen tree or shrub native to the Americas. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which help to treat a variety of health issues. The leaves, bark, and fruit of the Malpighia undulata plant have been used in herbal preparations to treat fever, diarrhea, and skin conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve digestion. Herbal preparations of Malpighia undulata can be found in the form of teas, tinctures, and infusions, and have been used to support overall health and well-being.
